ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic toast class (FOODON_00002683). No specific multigrain butter toast entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and similar products. No specific multigrain butter toast code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for multigrain butter toast.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving consists of two slices of toasted multigrain bread spread with butter, weighing approximately 80 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Multigrain bread, toasted with butter — composite estimate based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Bread, multi-grain (includes whole-grain) (NDB 18069) and Butter, salted (NDB 01001)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for multigrain butter toast in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded. Values estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy multigrain bread (toasted, ~10% moisture loss) with a standard butter application of approximately 8 g per 100 g of finished product. Toasting increases nutrient density per 100 g by approximately 10–12% relative to untoasted bread due to moisture loss. Butter contributes fat, saturated fat, and energy. Final per-100g values represent the composite toasted product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of multigrain and wholegrain breads — University of Sydney GI Database; reference food: multigrain bread, GI ~65 (glucose reference)
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values for multigrain bread in the range of 48–69 (glucose=100 reference). A central estimate of GI 65 is used for multigrain bread, consistent with published values for mixed-grain commercial breads. Butter addition has negligible effect on GI. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 39.6 g carbohydrate in 80 g serving) / 100 ≈ 25.7.

hypertension.guidance: Each 80 g serving provides approximately 312 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to one serving and avoid additional high-sodium accompaniments such as kaya or salted eggs.

type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 65 and a glycemic load of ~25.7 per serving, multigrain butter toast causes a moderate postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to one slice and pair with a protein source to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium from butter
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The butter component contributes approximately 3.4 g saturated fat per serving, which approaches 17% of the recommended daily limit; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consider reducing butter quantity or substituting with an unsaturated fat spread.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Multigrain Butter Toast is a classic Singaporean kopitiam (coffee shop) breakfast item consisting of two slices of multigrain bread that are toasted until golden and crisp, then spread generously with cold salted butter. The multigrain bread typically contains a blend of wheat, oats, seeds, and other grains, giving it a denser texture and nuttier flavour compared to white bread. The contrast between the warm, crisp toast and the cold, melting butter is a defining sensory characteristic of this dish. It is commonly served alongside soft-boiled eggs seasoned with soy sauce and white pepper, and a cup of kopi (local coffee) or teh (local tea). Nutritionally, multigrain butter toast provides moderate energy (~310 kcal per 100 g), meaningful dietary fibre (~4 g per 100 g), and B-vitamins from the grain component. The butter contributes saturated fat and sodium, making portion control relevant for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index of approximately 65 places it in the medium GI category, and the glycemic load per standard serving is approximately 25.7, indicating a moderate impact on blood glucose. This dish is a staple of Singaporean breakfast culture and is widely available at Toast Box, Ya Kun Kaya Toast, and independent kopitiams across the island.
common_user_queries: How many calories in multigrain butter toast?
Is multigrain toast healthy for diabetics?
What is the GI of multigrain bread?
Is Toast Box multigrain toast high in fat?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for multigrain butter toast was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy multigrain bread (NDB 18069) adjusted for toasting moisture loss and a standard butter application. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database range for multigrain breads. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in approved sources.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy components (multigrain bread toasted + butter) as no exact Singapore-specific entry was available in HPB Foodo at time of processing. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy multigrain bread entry and is not reliably contributed by butter at the quantities used. Vitamin C is 0.0 as multigrain bread and butter contain no meaningful vitamin C; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Trans fat of 0.1 g per 100 g reflects naturally occurring trans fats from dairy butter (vaccenic acid) as reported in USDA SR Legacy butter entry. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data. GI estimated at 65 from University of Sydney GI Database range for multigrain commercial breads (GI 48–69). Glycemic load per 80 g serving = (65 × 39.6) / 100 = 25.7. Halal status assumed based on standard commercial multigrain bread and butter ingredients; individual restaurant products should be verified. The food_name has been normalised from the input 'Multigrain Traditional Butter Toast' to the canonical form 'Multigrain Butter Toast'; the original name is preserved in aliases.
